hypothesis: If a single classifier-free guided diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state molecular dynamics data and, at inference, its reverse SDE is guided by score-based interpolation between endpoint states and then refined by score-aligned ascent along r_SAA = s^B - s^A, then it will generate transition-state ensembles with committor approximately 0.5 and outperform linear and geodesic interpolation baselines across diverse systems because the interpolated and differential scores approximate the transition-state manifold and reaction coordinate.
